channel.title: Craft
channel.description: Craft is a list of hand oriented craft and technology Do we really need all those power tools?

item.title: ShopNotes Podcast Episode 268 — A Fistful of Pearwood
item.description:
In this episode, we’re finding a woodworking fix for invasive Callery pear trees and catching up with the Woodsmith Shop and Popular Woodworking. Head to the Woodsmith website for more...

Source

Head to the Woodsmith website for more...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/shopnotes-podcast-episode-268-a-fistful-of-pearwood/)

item.title: LAP Open Wire, April 18, 2026
item.description: Comments are now closed – thanks to everyone who joined us today, and those reading later. Join us again on June 20! Got a woodworking question, a query about dog holes (which Kale is drilling above, for their new tail vise) or are curious to know what’s on the horizon at Lost Art Press? Today...
item.source:markdown: Comments are now closed – thanks to everyone who joined us today, and those reading later. Join us again on June 20! Got a woodworking question, a query about dog holes (which Kale is drilling above, for their new tail vise) or are curious to know what’s on the horizon at Lost Art Press? Today...

item.title: Tool + Accessory = A Better Tool
item.description:
Milwaukee has added more offerings in its ever-growing category of vacuum tool accessories, with a couple of them great for woodworkers. The company’s “Air-Tip” tools have always been targeted at the...

Source

The company’s “Air-Tip” tools have always been targeted at the...

item.title: Slow Cooker
item.description: I’ve been at home this week (and possibly will be next) staring on and off at my not-yet-finished kitchen – on which I cannot currently work – every time I go to refresh my ice pack and drink more fluids. (I’m recovering from minor but uncomfortable surgery, and am under doctor’s orders to do no...

item.title: Simple Router Table

item.title: A Back-saving Vise Extension
item.description:
While sawing dovetails for a jewelry box, I found my back was hurting from hunching over my bench vise. In order to work more comfortably, I built this vise extension...

Source

item.title: Applications Open for Chairmaker’s Toolbox Class
item.description: The Chairmaker’s Toolbox is accepting applications for a scholarship class this August at Lost Art Press. All six students will receive free tuition and materials (plus some meals) during the week-long chairmaking class, Aug. 3-7. Apply for this scholarship class here. Applications close on May 15. The class will be taught by Grace Ambrose and...
item.source:markdown: The Chairmaker’s Toolbox is accepting applications for a scholarship class this August at Lost Art Press. All six students will receive free tuition and materials (plus some meals) during the week-long chairmaking class, Aug. 3-7. Apply for this scholarship class here. Applications close on May 15. The class will be taught by Grace Ambrose and...

item.title: See My Chairs at the ‘No Coast Furniture Salone’
item.description: I’m exhibiting two stick chairs at the “No Coast Furniture Salone” that runs from Friday, April 17, through Sunday, May 3, in an exhibit space at 1800 Race Street in Cincinnati, Ohio. I’m showing two stick chairs in black cherry that are based on the 18th-century Bebb chair from Wales (one of the key chairs...
item.source:markdown: I’m exhibiting two stick chairs at the “No Coast Furniture Salone” that runs from Friday, April 17, through Sunday, May 3, in an exhibit space at 1800 Race Street in Cincinnati, Ohio. I’m showing two stick chairs in black cherry that are based on the 18th-century Bebb chair from Wales (one of the key chairs...

item.title: Super Sandpaper
item.description:
Whenever I was sanding by hand, the sandpaper would roll or slide in my hands, eventually tearing and turning into small, useless scraps. I tried folding it every which way...

item.title: Plaiting a Hammer Handle
item.description: While I was teaching a chairmaking class at the Woodworking School at Pinecroft last week, school manager Katie Bister offered to plait the handle of my Exeter hammer with the woody stalks of broom corn.  Katie is a multi-talented craftsperson in fiber arts and woodworking, so I immediately said yes. I offered her a Crucible...
item.source:markdown: While I was teaching a chairmaking class at the Woodworking School at Pinecroft last week, school manager Katie Bister offered to plait the handle of my Exeter hammer with the woody stalks of broom corn. Katie is a multi-talented craftsperson in fiber arts and woodworking, so I immediately said yes. I offered her a Crucible...

item.title: Revisiting Berea Student Craft
item.description: Like most Kentuckians, I’ve long been buying brooms and other crafts made by students working for Berea College Student Craft. The students aren’t craft majors. Instead they work for Berea Student Craft as part of their work requirement at the college (which does not charge tuition). And I’ve visited the Berea Student Craft department a...
item.source:markdown: Like most Kentuckians, I’ve long been buying brooms and other crafts made by students working for Berea College Student Craft. The students aren’t craft majors. Instead they work for Berea Student Craft as part of their work requirement at the college (which does not charge tuition). And I’ve visited the Berea Student Craft department a...

item.title: New Model Mandolin: 3
item.description:
Here’s Part 3 of my New Model Mandolin build series. In this one, I look at joining the Adirondack soundboard and making and fitting the rosette. There’s also a bit more on the neck and last but certainly not least, the back gets braced and fitted and you’ll see how the radius dish (seen in part 1) continues to help the process.

Cheers Gary
item.source:markdown:
Here’s Part 3 of my New Model Mandolin build series. In this one, I look at joining the Adirondack soundboard and making and fitting the rosette. There’s also a bit more on the neck and last but certainly not least, the back gets braced and fitted and you’ll see how the radius dish (seen in part 1) continues to help the process.
Cheers Gary
item.author: noreply@blogger.com (Gary Nava, Luthier)

item.title: Handworks 2026, Sept. 4 & 5 in Amana, Iowa

item.title: Now Shipping: ‘Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk’
item.description: Now available in our store is Ed Zappen’s book “Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk.” Inside, you’ll find construction drawings, details and techniques, as well as plenty of color photos, to guide you as you build your own faithful replica of the Declaration Desk. Plus, an overview of events that led to the American Revolution and some...
item.source:markdown: Now available in our store is Ed Zappen’s book “Thomas Jefferson’s Declaration Desk.” Inside, you’ll find construction drawings, details and techniques, as well as plenty of color photos, to guide you as you build your own faithful replica of the Declaration Desk. Plus, an overview of events that led to the American Revolution and some...

item.title: 112. Otto D'Ambrosio
item.description:
For episode 112 of the podcast, I'm talking with Otto D'Ambrosio.

Otto wears two hats—as a solo luthier and Chief Designer at Eastman Guitars—and he walks us through his journey from working with legends like Flip Scipio, John Monteleone, and Carl Thompson to his role today, bridging the gap between hands-on building and production design.
 
We talk about how the pandemic pushed him into 3D CAD, changing the way he collaborates with Eastman's workshops, and dive into some of his design work—including the Fullertone neck system, his boutique archtop builds, and an upcoming project comparing domestic and European tonewoods.

Links:
https://dambrosioguitars.com/

Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of Bashkin Guitars and brought to you by the Fretboard Journal. This episode is sponsored by the Looth Group, Dream Guitars and StewMac.

Michael Bashkin's Hub of Acoustics 2026 US Academy: https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado_Academy
item.source:markdown:
For episode 112 of the podcast, I'm talking with Otto D'Ambrosio.
Otto wears two hats—as a solo luthier and Chief Designer at Eastman Guitars—and he walks us through his journey from working with legends like Flip Scipio, John Monteleone, and Carl Thompson to his role today, bridging the gap between hands-on building and production design.
We talk about how the pandemic pushed him into 3D CAD, changing the way he collaborates with Eastman's workshops, and dive into some of his design work—including the Fullertone neck system, his boutique archtop builds, and an upcoming project comparing domestic and European tonewoods.
Links:
[https://dambrosioguitars.com/](https://dambrosioguitars.com/)
Luthier on Luthier is hosted by Michael Bashkin of [Bashkin Guitars](https://www.bashkinguitars.com) and brought to you by the [Fretboard Journal](https://shop.fretboardjournal.com/products/fretboard-journal-annual-subscription). This episode is sponsored by [the Looth Group](https://loothgroup.com), [Dream Guitars](https://www.dreamguitars.com/) and [StewMac](https://www.stewmac.com/?irclickid=VA-TmuXZ%3AxyPUn0Ut-05ZTupUkHUPAzGE2bmy00&utm_source=3755630&utm_medium=Impact&utm_campaign=3755630&utm_content=Online%20Tracking%20Link_1303370&irgwc=1&partner=Fretboard%20Journal&mpid=3755630&group=).
Michael Bashkin's Hub of Acoustics 2026 US Academy: [https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado\_Academy](https://hubofacoustics.com/en/#Colorado_Academy)

item.title: Holy Swedish Burls Batman
item.description:
Editor’s note: This article was originally written in December 2025, for the April 2026 issue, which went to print in January 2026. As we wrap up this issue of the...

Source
item.source:markdown:
![](https://media.popularwoodworking.com/app/uploads/2026/03/30102525/img_6848-copy-1024x768.jpg)Editor’s note: This article was originally written in December 2025, for the April 2026 issue, which went to print in January 2026. As we wrap up this issue of the...
[Source](https://www.popularwoodworking.com/editors-blog/holy-swedish-burls-batman/)
